Submodules#

puretorch.tensor module#

class puretorch.tensor.Tensor(data: int | float | list | tuple | ndarray | Variable, requires_grad: bool = False, grad_fn: Function | None = None, is_leaf: bool = True, device: str = 'cpu')[source]#

Bases: Variable

property device#
property item#
relu() → Tensor[source]#

Module contents#

class puretorch.Tensor(data: int | float | list | tuple | ndarray | Variable, requires_grad: bool = False, grad_fn: Function | None = None, is_leaf: bool = True, device: str = 'cpu')[source]#

Bases: Variable

property device#
property item#
relu() → Tensor[source]#
puretorch.all(x: Tensor) → bool[source]#

Return True if all elements of the tensor evaluate to True. Equivalent to np.all(x).

puretorch.allclose(a: Tensor, b: Tensor, **kwargs) → bool[source]#

Return True if two tensors are elementwise equal within a tolerance. kwargs are passed to np.allclose (rtol, atol, equal_nan).

puretorch.enable_grad()[source]#
puretorch.equal(a: Tensor, b: Tensor) → bool[source]#

Return True if two tensors have the same shape and elements. Equivalent to np.array_equal(a, b).

puretorch.linspace(*args, **kwargs) → Tensor[source]#

Creates np.ndarray and initializes puretorch.Tensor instance.

puretorch.make_dot(output_tensor, params: dict | None = None, filename: str = 'autograd_graph', directory: str | None = None, format: str = 'png', graph_attr=None, node_attr=None, edge_attr=None)[source]#

Experimental, this function is likely to change in later updates.

Draws trace for output_tensor.

Parameters:

  • output_tensor – the final Tensor (e.g., out) you called backward on.

  • params – optional dict {name: tensor} to highlight parameters.

puretorch.no_grad()[source]#
puretorch.tensor(*shape, **kwargs) → Tensor[source]#

Creates Tensor of given shape.

puretorch.zeros_like(a: Tensor) → Tensor[source]#

Returns a Tensor containing zeros, shaped like input (a).
